flows.name: Email + password
flows.summary: The reference sign-in surface: register, sign in, sign out, and read the current user against email + password credentials.

howto.provision:
# Provision the auth:email modules onto your database (see Backend below for the full list).
pgpm install   # or: provision via databaseProvisionModule.create({ data: { modules } })
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-sign-in-card @constructive/auth-sign-up-card @constructive/auth-sign-out-button @constructive/auth-sign-in-page @constructive/auth-sign-up-page

flows.name: Email verification
flows.summary: Confirm a user owns their email: a verify-link landing page plus a resend banner for unverified accounts.

howto.provision:
# auth:email already exposes verifyEmail / sendVerificationEmail — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-verify-email-banner @constructive/auth-verify-email-page

flows.name: Password reset
flows.summary: Forgot-password request plus the emailed reset-token landing — enumeration-safe by construction.

howto.provision:
# forgotPassword / resetPassword ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-forgot-password-card @constructive/auth-forgot-password-page @constructive/auth-reset-password-card @constructive/auth-reset-password-page
howto.wire:
// Both pages are thin route wrappers — no extra wiring beyond BlocksRuntime.
// forgot-password-page reads ?email=; reset-password-page reads ?token= and ?role_id=.

flows.name: Social / OAuth sign-in
flows.summary: Sign in with configured identity providers (Google, GitHub, …) rendered as a button row or a prominent grid.

howto.provision:
# auth:sso adds connected_accounts_module + identity_providers_module (see Backend below).
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-social-buttons @constructive/auth-social-providers-grid

flows.name: Cross-origin sign-in
flows.summary: Hand an authenticated session to another origin via a short-lived one-time token appended to the destination URL.

howto.provision:
# requestCrossOriginToken / signInCrossOrigin ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-cross-origin-link
howto.usage:
import { CrossOriginLink } from '@/blocks/auth/cross-origin-link/cross-origin-link';

<CrossOriginLink email={email} password={password} destinationOrigin="https://app.example.com" destinationPath="/auth/cross-origin" />

flows.name: Profile
flows.summary: Let the signed-in user edit their display name and avatar against the auth:email user model.

howto.provision:
# updateUser / currentUser ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-account-profile-card
howto.usage:
import { AccountProfileCard } from '@/blocks/auth/account-profile-card/account-profile-card';

<AccountProfileCard onSuccess={() => toast.success("Profile updated")} />

flows.name: Account emails
flows.summary: Manage the signed-in user's email addresses: add, verify, set primary, and remove.

howto.provision:
# emails_module + its CRUD ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-account-emails-list

flows.name: Change password
flows.summary: An authenticated, step-up-gated form to set a new password with an inline strength meter.

howto.provision:
# setPassword / checkPassword ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-change-password-form
howto.usage:
import { ChangePasswordForm } from '@/blocks/auth/change-password-form/change-password-form';

<ChangePasswordForm requireStepUp showPasswordStrength onSuccess={() => toast("Password updated")} />

flows.name: Sessions
flows.summary: List the user's active sessions and revoke them individually or in bulk, gated behind step-up.

howto.provision:
# sessions_module + revokeSession ship with auth:email — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-account-sessions-list

constraints: Single revoke is step-up tier=medium; revoke-all-others is tier=high — both must complete a step-up before the mutation fires.
No generated list hook for sessions — supply rows via the `sessions` prop; the block lists but does not fetch.
knownBackendLimitations: revokeSession is uncallable from the auth result: the id on a signUp/signIn result is a UUIDv5 identity/credential id, not the UUIDv7 sessions.id, and no field exposes the real session id — so revokeSession(authResult.id) returns SESSION_NOT_FOUND. Ship revoke-current-session as backend-pending; do NOT hand-craft a session id or fall back to SQL. (platform-gaps.md GAP-2)

flows.name: API keys
flows.summary: Create and revoke user-scoped API keys, with a one-time reveal modal and step-up on create.

howto.provision:
# API-key CRUD ships with auth:email (user_auth_module) — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-account-api-keys-list @constructive/auth-api-key-create-dialog @constructive/auth-api-key-created-modal

constraints: createApiKey accessLevel accepts ONLY { 'read_only', 'full_access' } — any other value (read/write/admin, required) fails with INVALID_ACCESS_LEVEL at runtime; the auth-api-key-create-dialog block ships an accessLevelOptions list (read/write/admin) that does NOT match the deployed proc, so constrain the UI to the two valid values.

createApiKey enforces STEP_UP_REQUIRED server-side: a verifyPassword on the SAME session must precede the create (defense-in-depth beyond the client gate). The dialog runs that step-up first; a direct createApiKey call must complete step-up before the mutation.
knownBackendLimitations: revokeApiKey returns true and writes an audit-log entry but never sets revoked_at — the key keeps working. Treat its true as a no-op, not proof of revocation; do not surface "revoked" as terminal state. (platform-gaps.md GAP-3)

flows.name: Account deletion
flows.summary: A danger-zone card that emails a deletion confirmation, plus the page that completes the deletion from the link.

howto.provision:
# delete_account flow ships with auth:email (user_auth_module) — no extra modules.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-account-danger-card @constructive/auth-account-deletion-confirm-page

flows.name: Step-up verification
flows.summary: Re-verify identity (password or TOTP) before a sensitive action, as a dialog or an imperative hook.

howto.provision:

pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/auth-step-up-dialog @constructive/use-step-up

# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh

flows.name: Organizations
flows.summary: Create and configure organizations — first-class User records (type=2) in the unified user model.

howto.provision:
# Orgs require the full B2B stack (org-scoped memberships/permissions/invites/hierarchy).
# There is no preset smaller than b2b for org flows — see Backend below.
pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/org-create-card @constructive/org-settings-form

flows.name: Org members
flows.summary: List an organization's members with inline role changes and step-up-gated removal.

howto.provision:

pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/org-members-list

flows.name: Org roles
flows.summary: Create, edit, and delete named org role profiles that bundle the org-scoped permission set.

howto.provision:

pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/org-create-card @constructive/org-roles-editor
howto.wire: // Both blocks bind to the generated admin SDK hooks — only BlocksRuntime is required.

pgpm install
howto.install:
# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
npx shadcn@latest add @constructive/org-invite-dialog @constructive/auth-invitation-acceptance-card @constructive/auth-invitation-acceptance-page

# first register @constructive against your served registry — see blocks-onramp §4c / run scripts/serve-registry.sh
